checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 1 screenshot confirms task.html exists in the Downloads folder; steps 2,3,9,10 show selecting Chrome as the application to open task.html and clicking 'Just once'; step 12 screenshot shows Chrome displaying the task.html page with the interactive 'Click Me' button and number 5, matching the checkpoint's required evidence of Chrome loading the task.html page with interactive elements.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 1 confirms the task.html file exists in the Downloads folder. Step 2 displays the 'Open with' dialog with Chrome as an available application. Step 10 shows selecting 'Just once' to open the file using Chrome. Step 11 provides conclusive evidence that Chrome has successfully loaded the task.html page, as indicated by the visible 'Memory Task' interface with the 'Click Me' interactive button, fulfilling the checkpoint's requirements.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Selected steps and screenshots verify two separate sequences of exactly 5 distinct clicks on the 'Click Me' button (steps 12-16 and steps 25-29), each followed by the appearance of the 'Enter the product' form (screenshots for steps 17 and 29), confirming the button was clicked exactly 5 times as required.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: These steps include the initial page load (steps 11, 24) displaying the 'Click Me' button, followed by 5 distinct click actions on the button in two separate attempts (steps 13-17, 25-29). The subsequent state in steps 17 and 29, where the 'Enter the product' form appears, verifies that exactly 5 clicks were completed, matching the checkpoint requirement.

checkpoint_results.evidence: The screenshots for steps 18, 21, 30, and the final state snapshot all show the form input field on the task.html page displays only the placeholder text 'Enter the product' with no numerical product entered, despite the agent attempting to type '1889472' in prior steps.
checkpoint_results.missing_or_conflict: The agent's claim of typing the product value '1889472' conflicts with the visible empty input field in all relevant screenshots, and there is no evidence of the correct numerical product being present in the form input field.

first_pass_result.evidence: The screenshot for step 30 shows the form input field on the task.html page still displays the placeholder text 'Enter the product' with no numerical product entered, despite the agent attempting to type '1889472' in prior steps. Additionally, all relevant screenshots of the form input field (steps 18, 21, 30) show the field remains empty or with placeholder text, confirming the product was not successfully entered.
first_pass_result.missing_or_conflict: The agent's claim of typing the product value '1889472' conflicts with the visible empty input field in the screenshots, and there is no evidence of the correct numerical product being present in the form input field.

checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: These steps document the numbers displayed after each button click (5,4,2,6), the transition to the form input field, the user's attempts to interact with the input field, type the product value '1889472', and submit, as well as the repeated attempt to reopen the file and navigate to the form. All relevant steps show the form input field retains the placeholder text 'Enter the product', confirming the product was not successfully entered into the field.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_fallback_reason: No step provides a screenshot showing the form input field containing the correct numerical product; the typing action did not register in the UI, and the input field remains empty or displays placeholder text in all relevant steps.
